report_name: Microsoft's May 2026 Patch Tuesday: 137 Flaws Fixed, Including Critical Netlogon RCE

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-41089
vulnerabilities.description: A critical Remote Code Execution (RCE) vulnerability in the Windows Netlogon service. An unauthenticated attacker on the same network can send a specially crafted request to a domain controller, potentially achieving remote code execution with SYSTEM privileges. The vulnerability is described as highly wormable.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-41096
vulnerabilities.description: A critical Remote Code Execution (RCE) vulnerability in the Windows DNS Client. An attacker controlling a malicious DNS server can execute code on a client machine that sends it a query. This could be triggered by a user browsing a malicious website or opening a malicious email.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-42826
vulnerabilities.description: A critical information disclosure vulnerability in Azure DevOps which could reveal highly sensitive secrets or credentials, potentially leading to a full compromise.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-42898
vulnerabilities.description: A critical Remote Code Execution (RCE) vulnerability affecting on-premise deployments of Microsoft Dynamics 365.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-40361
vulnerabilities.description: A remote code execution vulnerability in Microsoft Word that can be exploited via the Preview Pane in Outlook, allowing an attacker to achieve RCE without the user opening the malicious document.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-40464
vulnerabilities.description: A remote code execution vulnerability in Microsoft Word that can be exploited via the Preview Pane in Outlook, allowing an attacker to achieve RCE without the user opening the malicious document.

indicators.name: Potential CVE-2026-41089 Netlogon RCE Exploitation
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential CVE-2026-41089 Netlogon RCE Exploitation
id: 2b8f6c1d-9a3e-4b7c-8d5f-1a2b3c4d5e6f
status: experimental
description: Detects suspicious child processes spawning from the LSASS process on domain controllers, which could indicate exploitation of the Netlogon RCE vulnerability CVE-2026-41089.
references:
  - https://www.darkreading.com/vulnerabilities-threats/patch-tuesday-microsoft-not-a-zero-day-in-sight
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-13
tags:
  - attack.t1068
  - attack.privilege_escalation
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\lsass.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Highly unlikely. LSASS should not be spawning command shells.
level: critical
indicators.description: Detects suspicious child processes spawning from the LSASS process on domain controllers, which could indicate exploitation of the Netlogon RCE vulnerability CVE-2026-41089.

indicators.name: Potential CVE-2026-41096 DNS Client RCE Exploitation
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential CVE-2026-41096 DNS Client RCE Exploitation
id: 9e8d7c6b-5a4f-3e2d-1c0b-9a8b7c6d5e4f
status: experimental
description: Detects the DNS Client service (svchost.exe hosting Dnscache) spawning suspicious child processes, which could indicate exploitation of the DNS Client RCE vulnerability CVE-2026-41096.
references:
  - https://www.darkreading.com/vulnerabilities-threats/patch-tuesday-microsoft-not-a-zero-day-in-sight
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-13
tags:
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.execution
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\svchost.exe'
    ParentCommandLine|contains: 'Dnscache'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate but rare administrative actions. Should be investigated.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the DNS Client service (svchost.exe) spawning suspicious child processes, which could indicate exploitation of the DNS Client RCE vulnerability CVE-2026-41096.

indicators.name: Potential Office RCE via Preview Pane Exploitation
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Office RCE via Preview Pane Exploitation
id: 7a6b5c4d-3e2f-1d0c-9b8a-7f6e5d4c3b2a
status: experimental
description: Detects Microsoft Word or Outlook spawning suspicious child processes, which could indicate exploitation of vulnerabilities like CVE-2026-40361 or CVE-2026-40464 via the preview pane.
references:
  - https://www.darkreading.com/vulnerabilities-threats/patch-tuesday-microsoft-not-a-zero-day-in-sight
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-13
tags:
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.execution
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of macros or add-ins, but such behavior is highly suspicious and warrants investigation.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects Microsoft Word or Outlook spawning suspicious child processes, which could indicate exploitation of vulnerabilities like CVE-2026-40361 or CVE-2026-40464 via the preview pane.
